linting.notes: No linter configuration found. No checkstyle, PMD, spotbugs for Java. No flake8, pylint, black for Python.

testing.mock_patterns: Tests use simple unit testing, no complex mocking infrastructure observed
testing.notes: Only ccfd-fuse has actual tests (2 test classes, 3 tests total). ccfd-notification-service compiles but has no test sources. ccfd-service modules have test infrastructure but minimal tests. Python projects have no tests.

ci.notes: No CI/CD configuration found. No .github/workflows, .gitlab-ci.yml, Jenkinsfile, .tekton, or other CI configs.

build.system: Maven for Java, pip for Python
build.install_command: mvn clean install (for Java projects in containers/), pip install -r requirements.txt (for Python projects)
build.notes: Multi-language repository with independent container-based applications. Each subdirectory in containers/ is a separate project. Java projects: ccfd-fuse (Spring Boot + Camel), ccfd-notification-service (Quarkus), ccfd-service (Spring Boot + KIE Server with multiple sub-modules). Python projects: ccfd-modelfull and ccfd-seldon-usertask-model (ML models with scikit-learn). No top-level parent pom.xml tying projects together.

conventions.test_naming: Java: class names end with 'Test', methods annotated with @Test. Python: no tests found.
conventions.import_style: Java: standard package imports. Python: standard imports.
conventions.notes: This is a tutorial/demo repository for OpenDataHub fraud detection workflows. Primary focus is deployment and configuration rather than test coverage.

test_commands.notes: No tests to run (compiles successfully but no test sources)

container_recipe.notes: Java 8 required as specified in pom.xml files. Maven downloads dependencies on first run (takes ~2 minutes). Python projects not tested in container but have minimal dependencies.

validation.summary: Java tests validated successfully in Maven container. ccfd-fuse: 3 tests passed. ccfd-notification-service: builds OK, no tests. Python projects not tested.

gaps: No CI/CD configuration - no automated testing on pull requests
No linting configuration for Java or Python
Minimal test coverage - only 3 tests in ccfd-fuse, no tests in other Java projects
No Python tests despite having 2 Python ML model projects
No test documentation or testing guidelines
Projects are independent - no unified build or test strategy
Container build profiles exist in pom.xml but not validated (docker-maven-plugin, fabric8-maven-plugin)
ccfd-service has complex multi-module structure but minimal test coverage
